description_long: This house features a rectangular, two-story structure with a red-tiled gable roof and a prominent chimney on one side. The exterior walls are finished in a textured, off-white stucco with wooden trim around the windows and doors. The front facade displays a series of square and rectangular windows arranged in a mostly symmetrical pattern, along with two entry doors—one main and one secondary. One side of the house includes a partially open balcony or terrace area, enclosed by a low wall with decorative cutouts. The rear and side elevations have fewer windows, emphasizing privacy and structural simplicity. The overall design suggests a Mediterranean or Southern European influence, with practical, robust construction and minimal ornamentation.
description: A two-story, rectangular house with a red-tiled gable roof, off-white stucco walls, and wooden-trimmed windows and doors. Features include a prominent chimney, a partially open terrace, and a symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main facade with two entry doors, several square and rectangular windows, and a partially open terrace on the left side.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals a plain wall with three evenly spaced square windows and the sloped red-tiled roof.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a simpler facade with fewer windows, the chimney, and the continuation of the terrace area.
description_view_3: The right side view highlights the open terrace with two horizontal windows and the gable end of the roof.
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with a simple wooden door, no porch, and minimal decorative elements; secondary door and terrace access on the front facade.
